ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for cranberry cream cheese bun; mapped to generic bread roll food product (FOODON_00002683) as closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Bread and similar products) used as closest defensible category; no specific code exists for filled sweet buns with cream cheese and dried fruit.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this specific bakery product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cranberry cream cheese bun weighing approximately 80 g, as commonly sold in Singapore bakeries.

nutrient_source.title: Cranberry Cream Cheese Bun — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Cranberry Cream Cheese Bun (cr_id F05020034). All per-100g core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 7.5 g/100g based on typical cream cheese and butter contribution within the reported total fat of 13.47 g/100g; this sub-fraction is not separately reported in the HPB record and is documented as an estimate. Detailed fatty acid fractions, full micronutrient panel, potassium, cholesterol, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for F05020034; these fields are set to -1 accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cranberry cream cheese bun. GI estimated at 71 based on white bread / enriched soft bread roll analogue (GI ~70–75) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The refined wheat flour base dominates the glycemic response; cream cheese filling has negligible carbohydrate contribution and does not materially lower GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (71) × available carbohydrate per serving (43.1 g/100g × 80 g serving = 34.5 g) / 100 = 24.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (15.4 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.2 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 302 mg/100g is borderline elevated; saturated fat (~7.5 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g threshold and contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this bun due to its combination of high sugar, borderline sodium, and elevated saturated fat, all of which independently and synergistically elevate blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 15.4 g/100g exceeds the diabetes threshold (>15 g/100g with fibre <2 g/100g); total carbohydrate is 43.1 g/100g from refined flour; estimated GI of 71 indicates rapid postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this bun or limit to very small occasional portions, as its high sugar content, low fibre, and high-GI refined flour base will cause significant postprandial blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at ~7.5 g/100g from cream cheese and butter exceeds the 5 g/100g CVD threshold; trans fat estimated at ~0.1 g/100g; high sugar (15.4 g/100g) contributes to dyslipidaemia and endothelial dysfunction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this bun due to its high saturated fat content from cream cheese and butter, estimated trans fat, and high sugar, all of which adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cranberry Cream Cheese Bun is a soft, enriched wheat bread roll filled with cream cheese and dried cranberries, commonly sold in Singapore bakeries and cafes. The bun is made from a yeasted, butter-enriched dough baked until golden, yielding a pillowy, slightly sweet crumb. The cream cheese filling provides a tangy, creamy contrast to the chewy, sweetened dried cranberries, which contribute concentrated fruit sugar and a mild tartness. Per 100 g, the bun delivers 327 kcal, 8.3 g protein, 13.47 g fat (of which approximately 7.5 g is saturated fat from butter and cream cheese), and 43.1 g carbohydrate including 15.4 g sugar and only 1.2 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 302 mg/100g. The high sugar content combined with low fibre and a refined flour base results in a high estimated glycemic index (~71), making this a high-glycemic-load food. Clinically, this bun is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar, elevated saturated fat, borderline sodium, and low fibre profile. It is best consumed occasionally and in small portions by the general population. Common serving size is approximately one bun (80 g), providing approximately 262 kcal and 34.5 g available carbohydrate per serving.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020034) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat sub-fraction estimated from ingredient composition (cream cheese and butter) and documented as an estimate. GI estimated from analogous white bread / soft roll data from the University of Sydney GI Database and documented accordingly.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full micronutrient panel, potassium, cholesterol, and moisture data for this specific commercial bakery product are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F05020034 as of 2026-04-16; no supplementary approved source was available to fill these fields. saturated_fat_g (7.5 g/100g) is an estimate derived from the known saturated fat content of cream cheese (~20 g/100g) and butter (~51 g/100g) proportioned against the total fat of 13.47 g/100g; this value should be confirmed against a full fatty acid panel if available. trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100g) is a low-level estimate from butter content; not separately reported in HPB source. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate based on white bread / enriched soft bread roll from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for cranberry cream cheese bun was found in approved sources. Local-language names (Chinese simplified: 蔓越莓奶油芝士面包, Chinese traditional: 蔓越莓奶油芝士麵包, Malay: Roti Krim Keju Cranberry, Tamil: கிரான்பெரி கிரீம் சீஸ் பன், Hindi: क्रैनबेरी क्रीम चीज़ बन) are provided as descriptive translations for multilingual retrieval purposes; this product is sold in Singapore and local-language name fields are populated to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance. ontology.foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type set to closest_parent as no exact ontology code exists for this specific filled sweet bun product.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Added Chinese simplified (蔓越莓奶油芝士面包), Chinese traditional (蔓越莓奶油芝士麵包), Malay (Roti Krim Keju Cranberry), Tamil (கிரான்பெரி கிரீம் சீஸ் பன்), and Hindi (क्रैनबेरी क्रीम चीज़ बन) to local_names, aliases flat array, and aliases_by_language to satisfy G6 and Q8.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to use allowed enum values and satisfy G5.
Iteration 3: Expanded notes to explicitly document the reason and source consulted for every -1 field, satisfying G3 numeric hygiene requirements.
Iteration 2: Updated notes to document all -1 values and their basis.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types to address G5 failure.
Iteration 1: Initial enrichment from HPB source, GI estimation, health context, and LLM description added.
